title: The Open Web Is Shifting From An Audience Economy To A Context Economy (5 minute read)
content: The next phase of ad tech is shifting from knowing who someone is to understanding what's happening around them right now. Instead of relying on fixed audience segments built from past behavior, new bidding infrastructure lets ad auctions read signals like timing and nearby activity in real time. Containerizing that decision-making inside the auction itself cuts response times by roughly 80%, giving algorithms enough time to weigh context instead of just matching an ID to a segment. Advertising stops chasing who people are and starts responding to the moment they're actually in.

title: Anthropic's newest ad is creeping people out (3 minute read)
content: Anthropic's new ad, “There's hope in hard questions”, is getting backlash for its dark tone and imagery. It opens with a burning house and cuts to scenes of surveillance, homelessness, mining labor, and graveyards while asking questions about AI trust and control. The campaign mirrors Anthropic's usual positioning as the ethical alternative in AI, but many viewers say it feels heavy-handed.

title: How to use content pillars to build your social strategy (7 minute read)
content: The best-performing content pillar systems in 2026 work as concentric circles instead of a flat list of themes. A narrow core pillar builds algorithmic and audience authority faster than a broad one, while adjacent pillars share that same audience and add new angles without diluting it. A pillar only counts as adjacent if it still reinforces why someone followed the account in the first place, since anything broader just makes the feed generic.

title: How to measure the impact of AI search the right way (8 minute read)
content: AI search is driving real demand, but most teams still measure it through referral traffic, which rarely shows up. Only around 1% of users click AI citations, and much of that traffic is misattributed or shows up as direct, making last-click models unreliable. Instead, use an “AI visibility ladder” that tracks leading indicators like crawl rate and citation share. It should also look at quality signals like how a brand is positioned in answers, and lagging indicators like self-reported attribution and win rate.

title: SEO & AI Tip: In AI Overviews, being recommended is the goal, not being cited (2 minute read)
content: AI Overviews recommend brands based on commercial prompts, not informational content. Ranking for informational queries rarely pays off downstream in commercial visibility. Build content around high-value commercial queries and support each page with pricing, comparison, methodology, and brand information that helps AI justify recommending your business.

title: Allan (Tool)
content: Allan is an AI email platform for DTC brands that builds a different email for each recipient instead of sending one campaign to everyone. It selects subject lines, copy, and creative based on user behavior, and runs continuous tests across every send to optimize performance. A 30-day free trial is available.

title: Brandom: Why Fandom is the Future of AI Search (52 minute video)
content: AI search is moving away from brand-owned content toward third-party voices on Reddit, YouTube, and LinkedIn. LLMs tend to trust sources that sit further from brand control, which makes customer, employee, and community advocacy more important than blog content. Reddit is emerging as a key offsite channel, similar to what blogs were for SEO, but requires real participation instead of spam. The shift is also blurring lines across SEO, PR, and community, while measurement relies on signals like share of voice and citations.

title: A Subtle Google Ads Bidding Change (2 minute read)
content: Campaign targets now matter more than past overperformance, so review every target-based bidding campaign before Google changes bidding on August 17. Campaigns that currently exceed their target ROAS may lose efficiency because Google will optimize toward the set goal instead of continuing to outperform it. Raise targets in small steps when recent performance is more than 20% above the current goal, or set a higher custom target if stronger performance is realistic. Advertisers that care more about conversion volume than efficiency should consider switching to a maximize bidding strategy.

title: Could EA's new advertising platform open the floodgates for in-game ads? (3 minute read)
content: Six years after apologizing for intrusive ads in UFC 4 and disabling them, EA is betting that real-time, targeted placements can succeed where static pop-ups failed. The new platform is built to enhance gameplay rather than interrupt it, giving brands the ability to adjust messaging in real time and measure impact for the first time. However, most gaming ad spend still lives on mobile, where casual play tolerates interruption more easily. Console and PC audiences treat games as a more focused experience that ads have historically struggled to enter without friction.

title: Building the nation's largest soccer league (7 minute read)
content: Community creates stronger long-term growth than paid acquisition when the customer experience gives people a reason to return and invite others. The Footy League built scalable operations behind its community, including customer service, registration systems, sponsor-ready media, and local experiences that support national expansion without losing its identity. It uses paid marketing to attract first-time players, but newsletter marketing and word of mouth create higher retention and stronger referrals. Brand comes from the customer experience instead of visual identity, so values need to appear in everyday interactions such as onboarding, training, and support.

title: OpenAI's ChatGPT ads could miss $100 billion revenue target (1 minute read)
content: OpenAI's forecast assumes it will capture search ad budgets at scale and outperform every ad format in history.

title: AEO Play: Ramp's Help Center (1 minute read)
content: Ramp creates robust pages on its Help Center documentation, which is often cited by AI search in the B2B space.

title: OpenAI's first device will reportedly be a ‘humanlike' rechargeable speaker (2 minute read)
content: OpenAI is building a screenless AI companion speaker that uses ChatGPT capabilities to interact naturally with users.
